carriedBehavior: Canonical marketplace source parsing, staging, validation, atomic activation, persistent indexing, plugin lookup, local plugin source realpath jailing, and safe removal live under runtime/src/plugins/marketplace; CLI/add operations use the same parser grammar for URL manifests, GitHub shorthand, SSH/file/git URLs, and local paths, and git clone transports reject non-loopback HTTP.
CLI and PluginInstallationManager marketplace methods call the canonical PK-07 layer directly.

Marketplace manager helpers cover config persistence, seed-dir discovery from AGENC_PLUGIN_SEED_DIR, source registration/removal, declared-vs-materialized marketplace diffing/reconciliation, cache refresh, plugin lookup, auto-update flags, policy allow/block matching, graceful loading degradation, and empty-state reason selection.
Marketplace manifest loading rejects malformed plugin entries, traversal sources, unsupported source shapes, and duplicate plugin names instead of silently dropping invalid catalog data.

Remote marketplace helpers cover authenticated listing, installed-plugin listing, detail and skill fetches, install/uninstall mutations, HTTPS-by-default remote and legacy API transport with explicit loopback opt-in before auth headers can be sent, path-jailed local cache cleanup, remote installed-bundle reconciliation with stale-cache and old-version removal plus in-flight cache mutation guards, and explicit JSON response-shape validation for current and legacy endpoints.

Remote bundle helpers validate release metadata and local plugin/marketplace cache identity segments, revalidate HTTPS/loopback policy at the download boundary, cap download/extracted sizes, reject path traversal, link, truncated, and checksum-corrupt tar entries, verify extracted plugin manifest identity, and activate versioned cache installs atomically.

Startup sync helpers refresh curated marketplace snapshots through guarded git first, then HTTP zipball with ZIP entry CRC checks and embedded archive identity verification, then backup archive, gate one-shot remote plugin reconciliation on local curated prerequisites, recover stale locks, skip the startup marker when a separate remote bundle sync is already in flight, wire the live REPL startup caller to the AgenC-owned startup checks module with explicit trust/config/env state, register seed marketplaces from AGENC_PLUGIN_SEED_DIR by resolving each seed's known_marketplaces.json entries to the seed's current marketplaces/<name> or marketplaces/<name>.json content with autoUpdate forced off, reconcile declared marketplaces into the local marketplace cache with AppState install-status updates, mark refresh after partial startup success even if a later step fails, wire the startup remote path to the concrete remote bundle reconciler through caller-provided auth or the remote-auth layer's bearer headers, and degrade to existing validated snapshots when refresh fails.

Marketplace fetch guards centralize HTTPS/loopback policy, timeout/abort handling, stream cancellation on size-limit failures, bounded response reads, and credential-redacted URL error reporting for URL manifests, remote API JSON, remote bundles, and startup metadata.
intentionalReductions: Auth token vending remains outside PK-07; remote functions accept caller-provided RemoteAuth headers and live startup can derive bearer headers from the remote-auth layer's persisted/bootstrap token state without reading API key environment variables directly.
Signed bundle verification is not invented in this row. PK-07 implements transport, path, and size safety, leaving signature policy to the later plugin security row.
Dependency solving, plugin demotion, and managed update orchestration are documented from inspected donors but are not wired into install/update decisions here.

Full plugin reload/MCP reconnect after startup marketplace reconciliation remains owned by the existing reload/registration path. PK-07 carries marketplace install-status updates and marks plugins for refresh when startup changes local plugin or marketplace cache state.
The donor GCS mirror path is reduced to AgenC-owned agenc.tech startup HTTP and backup archive fallbacks; no donor bucket or donor product domain is retained.
Already-merged PK-06 CLI marketplace modules are not deleted. Live callers import the canonical PK-07 layer directly and no compatibility wrapper is added.

commands: npm exec --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime vitest run src/plugins/marketplace src/plugins/cli/pluginCliCommands.test.ts
evidence: runtime/src/plugins/marketplace/ contains counterparts for every PK-07 named donor file.
runtime/src/plugins/marketplace/marketplace.ts calls parseMarketplaceInput during addMarketplaceOp, so CLI/add source syntax cannot drift from parser tests.

runtime/src/plugins/marketplace/marketplace.ts enforces HTTPS/loopback policy on marketplace git clone URLs and plugin git source URLs before exposing installable sources, and rejects symlink-resolved local plugin sources that escape the marketplace root.

runtime/src/plugins/marketplace/fetchGuards.ts provides shared timeout, stream cancellation, bounded read, URL policy, and redaction helpers used by URL manifests, remote APIs, bundles, and startup sync.

runtime/src/plugins/marketplace/remote.ts exposes syncRemoteInstalledPluginBundlesOnce/syncRemoteInstalledPluginBundles and markRemotePluginCacheMutationInFlight; regression tests install a remote bundle, prune older version directories, remove stale cache entries, skip guarded cache mutations, dedupe concurrent syncs by cache root, reject authenticated loopback HTTP remote API calls by default, and prove loopback HTTP requires explicit opt-in.
runtime/src/plugins/marketplace/remote_bundle.ts rejects invalid backend-provided pluginName/marketplaceName cache identity segments before computing plugin IDs or install paths.

runtime/src/plugins/marketplace/startup_sync.ts applies HTTPS/loopback transport policy before any curated git ls-remote or clone command, verifies ZIP entry CRCs, and verifies the extracted HTTP archive git identity before writing the advertised SHA.

runtime/src/plugins/marketplace/startup_remote_sync.ts can run through the concrete remote bundle reconciler when callers provide RemotePluginServiceConfig/RemoteAuth instead of an injected sync callback and does not write the startup marker when a separate remote bundle sync is already in flight.

runtime/src/plugins/marketplace/startup_checks.ts is the live REPL startup implementation; runtime/src/agenc/upstream/screens/REPL.tsx imports it directly, passes checkHasTrustDialogAccepted()/getGlobalConfig()/process.env, and the old upstream performStartupChecks file has been removed so startup no longer routes through the upstream marketplace utilities.

runtime/src/plugins/marketplace/startup_sync.test.ts covers trust-denied startup with no filesystem/network install work, AGENC_PLUGIN_SEED_DIR registration from seed known_marketplaces.json, declared marketplace reconciliation with AppState installation status, refresh signaling after partial startup failure, HTTP archive SHA mismatch and same-size CRC corruption rejection, no marker after in-flight remote bundle sync skips, live remote startup sync using auth-layer bearer headers from AGENC_REMOTE_AUTH_TOKEN, and live startup refusing to send those headers to loopback HTTP without explicit opt-in.
runtime/src/auth/backends/remote.contract.test.ts covers persisted and bootstrap-token remote auth header resolution for startup integrations.

runtime/src/types/upstream-ambient.d.ts is a narrow type-only contract for inherited upstream-mirror imports that are already in the tsc graph. It covers the exact inherited declaration gaps for src/entrypoints/agentSdkTypes.js, src/types/hooks.js, src/utils/model/model.js, src/utils/settings/constants.js, src/utils/settings/types.js, src/services/mcp/types.js, Bun.which/embeddedFiles, StructuredSerializeOptions.transfer, BodyInit, and OpenTelemetry forceFlush/shutdown provider methods without adding runtime compatibility exports or widening the typecheck baseline.
